title: Generalized Sharpe variance (AR(1) term) + 2026 reporting standard (MinTRL/Power/pFDR/oFDR)
source: LdP newer-corpus verification (4 parallel read-only agents over the added 2012-2026 papers + LdP REFERENCE CODE in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs/code, 2026-06-03). Conversation: 'verifica la nueva informacion antes de implementar'.
why: The 2026 'Sharpe Ratio Inference: a New Standard' does NOT retire PSR/DSR (it endorses them as central) but adds: (1) a GENERALIZED Sharpe variance with a first-order autocorrelation (AR(1) rho) term (LdP-Lipton-Zoonekynd 2025) — our PSR is the rho=0 (Mertens 2012) special case; ignoring positive serial correlation underestimates SR variance ('actual variance can be 4+ times larger'); (2) a recommended REPORTING bundle: MinTRL, Power, pFDR, oFDR alongside PSR/DSR.
value_rationale: Brings PSR/DSR to the formal 2026 standard. Likely modest impact on XAU D1 (per-trade pnl near-iid, low autocorrelation) but cheap and correct; the reporting bundle improves promotion-gate transparency.
scope: Add optional AR(1) rho term to the PSR variance denominator (generalized Sharpe variance); rho=0 reproduces current formula exactly
Estimate rho from the per-trade pnl series (likely small for XAU D1 — verify)
Report MinTRL, Power, pFDR, oFDR in psr_dsr.json per the 2026 standard reporting table
recommendation: Low urgency on XAU D1 (near-iid pnl) but adopt for standard-compliance; verify rho is small before weighting it.

external_refs.label: generalized Sharpe variance AR(1) + reporting standard

history.reason: verification: 2026 standard adds AR(1) Sharpe variance + MinTRL/Power/pFDR/oFDR reporting
